risk.title: Risk Disclosures
risk.subtitle: Trading financial instruments involves significant risk. Please read and understand the following risk disclosures in full before opening a live account.
risk.warning_text: CFD and Forex trading involves a high degree of risk. It is possible to lose all of your invested capital. You should only trade with money you can afford to lose.

forex.pair_categories_desc: Olla Trade offers access to all three Forex pair categories.
forex.major_desc: The most traded currency pairs globally, all including the USD. Deepest liquidity, tightest spreads.
forex.minor_desc: Cross-currency pairs that exclude USD. Well-traded during regional sessions with good liquidity.
forex.exotic_desc: A major currency paired with an emerging market currency. Higher spreads and volatility.
forex.conditions_note: Spreads are variable. Figures shown are indicative under normal market conditions.
forex.peak_liquidity_desc: occurs during the London-New York overlap (15:00-19:00 GMT+2). EUR/USD, GBP/USD, and USD/JPY typically see the tightest spreads and largest volumes.
forex.pip_definition: A pip (percentage in point) is the smallest standard price movement in Forex. For most pairs, 1 pip = 0.0001. For JPY pairs, 1 pip = 0.01.
forex.spread_definition: The spread is the difference between the bid (sell) price and the ask (buy) price. Variable spreads reflect live market conditions.
forex.pip_example: EUR/USD Bid: 1.08420 | Ask: 1.08425 = 0.5 pip spread. If you buy 1 lot (100,000 units), 1 pip = $10 profit or loss.

drivers.label: Interest Rate Decisions
drivers.desc: Central bank rate changes are the single biggest mover of currency pairs. Higher rates attract capital inflows.

drivers.label: Inflation Data (CPI/PPI)
drivers.desc: Inflation prints directly influence central bank expectations. A hotter-than-expected CPI often boosts the base currency.

drivers.label: Employment Reports
drivers.desc: Non-Farm Payrolls (US), unemployment rate, and jobs data reflect economic health and drive USD pairs significantly.

drivers.label: GDP Growth
drivers.desc: Stronger economic output supports a currency's value. GDP beats vs expectations can trigger sharp moves in major pairs.

drivers.label: Political & Geopolitical
drivers.desc: Elections, sanctions, and geopolitical conflicts create uncertainty that can sharply move safe-haven currencies.

drivers.label: Trade Balance
drivers.desc: A country's export/import balance reflects demand for its currency. Persistent deficits often weigh on currency value.

why_features.title: Deepest Liquidity
why_features.desc: Over $6 trillion traded daily - enter and exit positions at any time with minimal price impact.

why_features.title: 24/5 Market Access
why_features.desc: Trade from Monday's Asian open through Friday's New York close across all four major sessions.

why_features.title: Tight Spreads
why_features.desc: Major pairs like EUR/USD from 0.0 pips - one of the most cost-efficient markets for retail traders.

why_features.title: Go Long or Short
why_features.desc: CFD structure lets you profit from both rising and falling currency prices.

why_features.title: EA-Friendly
why_features.desc: Deep liquidity and tight spreads make Forex ideal for automated Expert Advisor strategies on MT4.

why_features.title: 70+ Pairs
why_features.desc: Trade majors, minors, and exotic pairs - each with different volatility, spread, and session characteristics.

glossary_terms.def: Smallest price movement. 0.0001 for most pairs (0.01 for JPY pairs).
1/10th of a pip. Many brokers quote prices to 5 decimal places.
Difference between bid and ask price. Represents the cost of opening a trade.
Standard lot = 100,000 units. Mini lot = 10,000. Micro lot = 1,000.
Collateral required to open a leveraged position. Expressed as a percentage of position size.
Overnight financing cost or credit applied to positions held past market close.
Difference between expected and actual execution price. Common during news events.

indices.overview_desc: Each index tracks a specific basket of stocks and reflects the performance of a market, economy, or sector. Trading index CFDs gives you exposure without purchasing individual shares.
indices.conditions_note: Index CFD prices are derived from the underlying exchange. Trading hours follow primary exchange sessions. Spreads may widen at open/close.
indices.movers_desc: Index prices are driven by a combination of macroeconomic data, corporate performance, central bank policy, and global risk sentiment.
indices.comparison_title: Index CFDs vs ETFs — Key Differences
indices.comparison_desc: Both products track index performance, but with important structural differences for active traders.
indices.why_subtitle: Index CFDs offer broad market exposure with built-in diversification and the ability to trade in both directions.
indices.risk_text: Index CFD trading involves risk of loss. Leverage amplifies both gains and losses. Markets may gap on open, particularly after weekend closures or major overnight events.

index_data.name: US30 — Dow Jones
index_data.desc: The oldest US index, tracking 30 blue-chip companies. Despite its smaller basket, it remains one of the world's most widely followed market indicators.

index_data.name: NAS100 — Nasdaq 100
index_data.desc: Technology-heavy index covering companies like Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, and Meta. Highly sensitive to interest rates and tech sector performance.

index_data.name: SPX500 — S&P 500
index_data.desc: The broadest US benchmark — 500 companies spanning all sectors. Widely regarded as the definitive gauge of US equity market performance.
index_data.composition: 500 large US companies across all sectors

index_data.name: DAX40
index_data.desc: Germany's flagship index. Key European market indicator. Sensitive to EUR/USD, global trade conditions, and European Central Bank policy.

index_data.name: FTSE100
index_data.desc: The UK's benchmark index. Internationally exposed — many components earn revenues globally, making it sensitive to GBP strength and commodity prices.

index_data.name: NASDAQ
index_data.desc: Broader than NAS100, covering all Nasdaq listings. Reflects the performance of the entire Nasdaq exchange — tech-heavy but more diversified.

market_movers.desc: Interest rate decisions from the Fed, ECB, and BoE directly affect equity valuations. Rate cuts are generally bullish for indices; rate hikes can weigh on equities.
Quarterly earnings seasons drive significant index moves. Beats vs expectations from major index components can cause sharp directional moves.
GDP growth, employment figures, and manufacturing PMI data reflect economic health and directly influence equity market sentiment.
Trade wars, military conflicts, and political instability create risk-off sentiment, typically pressuring equity indices lower.
A weaker domestic currency often benefits export-heavy indices (DAX, FTSE). Currency strength can weigh on internationally-exposed companies.
Energy and materials prices significantly impact commodity-heavy indices like the FTSE100. Oil price moves affect energy sector weighting substantially.

why_features.title: Broad Market Exposure
why_features.desc: Trade the performance of entire economies or sectors with a single instrument — no need to analyse individual stocks.

why_features.title: Event-Driven Moves
why_features.desc: Earnings seasons, central bank decisions, and geopolitical events create significant index volatility and trading opportunity.

why_features.title: Long or Short
why_features.desc: Go long if you expect markets to rise, or short if you anticipate a market downturn — full directional flexibility.

why_features.title: Good Liquidity
why_features.desc: Major indices are among the most liquid instruments during their primary sessions — tight spreads and fast fills.

why_features.title: Leverage Available
why_features.desc: Access index positions with up to 1:100 leverage — control larger positions with a fraction of full capital.

why_features.title: Transparent Pricing
why_features.desc: Index prices are derived from the weighted basket of underlying stocks — fully transparent and trackable.

energies.instruments_desc: Brent and WTI are the world's two primary crude oil benchmarks. Both are quoted in USD per barrel and accessible as CFDs — speculate on price movements without physical delivery.
energies.brent_desc: Brent Crude is extracted from the North Sea and serves as the global oil pricing benchmark. It represents over two-thirds of internationally traded crude oil contracts. Brent is the reference price for oil produced in Europe, Africa, and the Middle East.
energies.wti_desc: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) is the primary North American crude oil benchmark, traded on the NYMEX. It is lighter and sweeter than Brent, extracted from US shale basins.
comparison_rows: ~65% of world oil priced on Brent
Usually trades at premium to WTI
Usually at slight discount to Brent
US rig count, EIA inventories, USD
energies.calendar_desc: These recurring events regularly create significant volatility in oil prices. Traders use them as anchors for scheduling positions and managing risk around data releases.

drivers.title: OPEC+ Production Quotas
drivers.desc: OPEC+ member countries collectively manage global supply. Production cut or increase decisions have immediate and direct impact on crude oil prices.

drivers.title: US EIA Inventory Reports
drivers.desc: Weekly US crude oil inventory data (published every Wednesday) is one of the most closely watched short-term price catalysts for WTI and Brent.

drivers.title: Geopolitical Risk
drivers.desc: Conflicts, sanctions, and political instability in major oil-producing regions (Middle East, Russia) create supply disruption concerns and price spikes.

drivers.title: USD Correlation
drivers.desc: Oil is priced in US Dollars globally. A stronger USD reduces purchasing power for non-USD buyers, typically creating downward pressure on oil prices.

drivers.title: Global Demand Outlook
drivers.desc: Economic growth data from China (world's largest oil importer) and the US directly influences energy demand projections and price direction.

drivers.title: Seasonal Demand Cycles
drivers.desc: Driving season (US summer), heating oil demand (winter), and refinery maintenance cycles create predictable seasonal patterns in energy markets.

oil_calendar.note: Most closely watched US oil supply data. Large inventory builds typically weigh on WTI prices.
Production quota decisions. Surprise cuts are generally bullish; increases bearish for oil.
Private sector estimate, released night before EIA. Provides early directional signal.
Indicator of US production activity. Rising rig count may signal future supply increases.
Crude oil import volumes from China reflect demand trends from the world's largest importer.
Strong jobs data suggests economic growth and higher energy demand; weak data implies lower demand.

crypto.how_desc: Speculate on the price movements of cryptocurrencies without owning the underlying coins. No wallet, no exchange account — trade directly from MT4.
crypto.instruments_desc: Each cryptocurrency has distinct characteristics, use cases, and price drivers. Understanding what moves each asset helps with strategy selection.
crypto.conditions_note: Crypto markets are highly volatile. Spreads may widen significantly during low-liquidity or high-volatility periods. Crypto CFDs trade Mon–Fri only. Weekend price gaps may occur.
crypto.risks_desc: Crypto CFDs carry risks beyond standard CFD trading. Understanding these risks before trading is essential.
crypto.read_full_risk: For responsible crypto trading, read our

cryptos.name: Bitcoin
cryptos.desc: Bitcoin is the world's first and most recognised cryptocurrency. Created in 2009, it operates on a deflationary model with a capped supply of 21 million coins. Bitcoin's price is driven by institutional adoption, halving cycles, macroeconomic sentiment, and regulatory developments.

cryptos.name: Ethereum
cryptos.desc: Ethereum is the leading smart contract platform, enabling decentralised applications (dApps), DeFi protocols, and NFT infrastructure. ETH's price is influenced by network activity, developer adoption, protocol upgrades, and its transition to proof-of-stake.

cryptos.name: Litecoin
cryptos.desc: Litecoin was one of the first alternative cryptocurrencies to Bitcoin. It offers faster transaction processing and uses a different hashing algorithm. It often follows Bitcoin's price trajectory with higher volatility.
keyDrivers: Network adoption and payment use cases

cryptos.name: Ripple
cryptos.desc: XRP is designed for fast, low-cost international payments and settlement. Its price has historically been influenced by regulatory developments and institutional adoption of the XRP Ledger for cross-border payment rails.

crypto_risks.label: Extreme Volatility
crypto_risks.desc: Crypto prices can move 10–30% in a single day. Position sizing and stop-loss orders are critical risk management tools.

crypto_risks.label: 24/7 Underlying Market
crypto_risks.desc: While Olla Trade crypto CFDs trade Mon–Fri, the underlying crypto markets trade 24/7. Significant weekend price gaps may occur when markets reopen Monday.

crypto_risks.label: Regulatory Uncertainty
crypto_risks.desc: Cryptocurrency regulation varies significantly by jurisdiction and continues to evolve. Regulatory announcements can trigger sharp price movements.

crypto_risks.label: Liquidity Variation
crypto_risks.desc: Crypto liquidity can vary significantly between altcoins. During market stress, spreads may widen substantially even on major pairs.

crypto_risks.label: No Physical Ownership
crypto_risks.desc: Crypto CFDs do not involve ownership of actual cryptocurrency. You cannot transfer, store, or use crypto CFD positions as real coins.

crypto_risks.label: Leverage Amplification
crypto_risks.desc: Even conservative 1:10 leverage on a volatile 15% crypto move can result in a 150% loss on margin. Only risk capital you can afford to lose entirely.

stocks.coverage_desc: Access 1,000+ stocks from major exchanges across three regions — all from your single Olla Trade MT4 account.
stocks.us_examples: Apple, Tesla, Amazon, Google, Microsoft, Meta, Netflix, Nvidia, JPMorgan, Visa
stocks.eu_examples: HSBC, BP, Shell, Volkswagen, Siemens, LVMH, Airbus, SAP, Unilever, ASML
stocks.asia_examples: Alibaba, Tencent, Samsung, TSMC, Toyota, Sony, BHP, Commonwealth Bank
stocks.conditions_note: Full instrument list available in MT4. Stock CFD availability may vary. Trading follows underlying exchange hours.
stocks.workflow_desc: Stock CFDs track the underlying company's share price. You speculate on price movement without owning shares, registering with a stock exchange, or waiting for settlement.

how_cfd_works.title: Choose a Stock
how_cfd_works.desc: Select from 1,000+ global stocks available in your MT4 instrument list. Search by company name or ticker symbol.

how_cfd_works.title: Select Direction
how_cfd_works.desc: Decide whether you expect the stock's price to rise (Buy/Long) or fall (Sell/Short). CFDs allow both directions equally.

how_cfd_works.title: Set Position Size
how_cfd_works.desc: Choose your lot size (minimum 0.01 lots). Your required margin is automatically calculated based on leverage and position size.

how_cfd_works.title: Set Risk Controls
how_cfd_works.desc: Apply a Stop Loss to cap your maximum loss and a Take Profit to lock in gains at a target price. Both are optional but recommended.

how_cfd_works.title: Monitor Position
how_cfd_works.desc: Track your open position in real-time via MT4. Floating P&L updates live as the stock price moves.

how_cfd_works.title: Close the Trade
how_cfd_works.desc: Close manually when your target is reached, or allow Stop Loss / Take Profit to close automatically at predefined levels.

stocks.sectors_desc: Different sectors respond to different economic conditions. Understanding sector dynamics helps with stock selection and timing.

sectors.examples: Apple, Microsoft, Nvidia, AMD, Salesforce, Adobe
sectors.note: Highly sensitive to interest rates, Fed policy, and earnings growth expectations.

sectors.examples: Amazon, Tesla, Nike, McDonald's, Starbucks, Netflix
sectors.note: Driven by consumer spending data, economic growth, and disposable income trends.

sectors.examples: JPMorgan, Visa, Bank of America, Goldman Sachs
sectors.note: Sensitive to interest rate direction, credit conditions, and regulatory environment.
Driven by clinical trial results, drug approvals, and healthcare policy changes.
Closely correlated with crude oil and natural gas prices.

sectors.examples: Meta, Alphabet, T-Mobile, Walt Disney, Comcast
sectors.note: Advertising revenue trends, streaming adoption, and platform regulatory risk.

stocks.dividend_long: If you hold a long position at the ex-dividend date, a dividend adjustment credit is applied to your account (equivalent to the dividend amount per share).
stocks.dividend_short: If you hold a short position, a dividend debit is applied to your account — reflecting the obligation a short seller would have on the underlying shares.
stocks.dividend_note: Dividend adjustments are made at end-of-day on the ex-dividend date and appear in the account statement. The amount depends on your position size and the declared dividend.
stocks.why_subtitle: Combine the price exposure of equity markets with the flexibility and efficiency of CFD trading.

why_features.title: No Share Ownership Needed
why_features.desc: Trade Apple, Tesla, Amazon and others on price movement — no share registry, no custodian, no settlement delay.

why_features.title: Long or Short
why_features.desc: Go long when you anticipate price rises; go short when you expect declines — full two-directional trading flexibility.

why_features.title: Leverage on Equities
why_features.desc: Control larger stock positions with a fraction of the capital using leverage up to 1:10 on eligible stocks.

why_features.title: Global Coverage
why_features.desc: 1,000+ stocks across US, UK, European, and Asian markets from a single Olla Trade MT4 account.

why_features.title: No Commission Structure
why_features.desc: As CFDs, no traditional brokerage commission applies — the cost is embedded in the spread only.

why_features.title: Earnings Season Trading
why_features.desc: Company results, product launches, and corporate announcements create significant short-term price opportunities.

forex.a: Forex (foreign exchange) trading involves buying one currency while simultaneously selling another. Currencies are traded in pairs such as EUR/USD. With Olla Trade, you can trade 70+ currency pairs as CFDs on MetaTrader 4.

forex.q: Which forex pairs are available on Olla Trade?
forex.a: Olla Trade offers more than 70 currency pairs across major, minor, and exotic categories. Major pairs include EUR/USD, GBP/USD, USD/JPY, AUD/USD, and USD/CHF.

forex.q: What are the spreads on forex pairs?
forex.a: Spreads on major forex pairs start from 0.0 pips on the Elite account and from 1.4 pips on the Standard account. Spreads are variable and may widen during periods of high volatility or low liquidity.

forex.q: Can I trade forex on MetaTrader 4?
forex.a: Yes. All Olla Trade accounts include full access to the MetaTrader 4 platform across desktop, WebTrader, iOS, and Android.
Forex prices are influenced by central bank rate decisions, GDP, CPI, employment data, geopolitical events, trade balances, and overall market risk sentiment.

metals.q: What metals can I trade with Olla Trade?
metals.a: Olla Trade offers trading on Gold (XAUUSD) and Silver (XAGUSD) as CFDs on the MetaTrader 4 platform.

metals.q: Can I trade gold and silver on MetaTrader 4?
metals.a: Yes. Gold (XAUUSD) and Silver (XAGUSD) are fully supported on the MT4 platform through your Olla Trade account.

metals.q: What affects gold and silver prices?
metals.a: Precious metals prices are influenced by USD strength, Federal Reserve policy, geopolitical uncertainty, central bank reserve purchases, inflation data, and global risk sentiment.
Yes. Gold (XAUUSD) can be traded with leverage of up to 1:200, and Silver (XAGUSD) with leverage of up to 1:100.

metals.q: What are the trading hours for metals?
metals.a: Gold and Silver are available 24 hours a day, Monday to Friday. The market is closed on weekends.

conditions.q: What is the minimum deposit at Olla Trade?
conditions.a: The minimum deposit is $10 for the Standard account, $2,000 for the Pro account, and $20,000 for the Elite account.

conditions.q: What leverage does Olla Trade offer?
conditions.a: Olla Trade offers leverage up to 1:500 on Forex pairs. Metals at up to 1:200, Indices and Energies at 1:100, and Crypto and Stocks at 1:10.
All spreads at Olla Trade are variable. They reflect live market conditions and may widen during periods of high volatility or low liquidity.
Standard and Pro accounts have no per-lot commission. The Elite account charges $3.5 per lot per side in exchange for raw spreads from 0.0 pips.

conditions.q: What is the margin call and stop out level?
conditions.a: The margin call level is 80%. When equity falls to 20% of required margin (stop out level), positions may begin to close automatically.
Yes. Olla Trade applies negative balance protection to all client accounts.

conditions.q: Can I use Expert Advisors (EAs) on Olla Trade?
conditions.a: Yes. Expert Advisors, automated trading, scalping, hedging, and all standard MT4 approaches are permitted on all account types.

contact.q: How can I contact Olla Trade support?
contact.a: Email cst@ollatrade.com or info@ollatrade.com. Our team is available Monday to Friday, 24 hours a day (24/5).

contact.q: How do I request a withdrawal?
contact.a: Log in to your client portal, go to Funding and Withdrawals, select Withdraw, choose your method and submit. Processing takes 24-48 business hours.

contact.q: How do I become an Olla Trade partner?
contact.a: Email info@ollatrade.com or visit our Partner Programme page.

funding.a: Log in to your client portal, navigate to Funding, select your preferred crypto method, and follow the on-screen instructions to generate a deposit address.

funding.q: How do I request a withdrawal?
funding.a: Log in to your portal, go to Funding and Withdrawals, select Withdraw, choose your method, enter the amount, and submit your request.
Olla Trade supports USDT (TRC20 and ERC20), Bitcoin (BTC), and Ethereum (ETH).
KYC verification is a legal requirement. It protects you from fraudulent withdrawal attempts.

funding.q: Can I withdraw my bonus credit?
funding.a: Bonus trading credit cannot be withdrawn directly. Only trading profits may be withdrawable, subject to the relevant promotion terms.

vps.q: What is a VPS for trading?
vps.a: A Virtual Private Server (VPS) is a remote computing environment that runs 24/7. For Forex traders, it provides a stable always-on environment to run MT4 and Expert Advisors without interruption.

vps.q: Why do I need a VPS for automated trading?
vps.a: A VPS ensures your Expert Advisors run continuously, even when your computer is off, without internet, or during power outages.

vps.q: Is VPS provided by Olla Trade?
vps.a: Olla Trade does not provide VPS services directly. This page provides guidance on setting up your own VPS with a third-party provider.

vps.q: What VPS specification should I use for MT4?
vps.a: For MT4 with a few EAs: minimum 1GB RAM (recommended 2GB+), 1 CPU core (recommended 2+), 20GB SSD, and stable 10Mbps+ connection.

vps.q: How do I access my VPS remotely?
vps.a: Use Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) on Windows, or Microsoft Remote Desktop on Mac/iOS with the IP address from your VPS provider.

alerts.a: Trading alerts are automated notifications that fire when a pre-set condition is met - such as a price reaching a level, an indicator crossing a threshold, or a time condition triggering.

alerts.q: How do I set up alerts in MetaTrader 4?
alerts.a: On MT4 desktop: right-click any price level on a chart and select Set Alert. On MT4 mobile: tap and hold on the chart and select Set Alert.

alerts.q: How do I receive push notifications on my phone?
alerts.a: Install the MT4 mobile app, note your MetaQuotes ID in the app settings, then in MT4 desktop go to Tools > Options > Notifications and enter your MetaQuotes ID.
No. Alerts in MT4 are informational only - they do not execute trades automatically. For automated trading, you would need an Expert Advisor (EA).

indices.a: Index CFDs allow you to speculate on the price movement of a stock market index — such as the Dow Jones (US30), S&P 500, NASDAQ, or DAX 40 — without purchasing the underlying shares.

indices.q: Which global indices are available on Olla Trade?
indices.a: Olla Trade offers CFDs on major global indices including US30, NASDAQ, SPX500 (S&P 500), DAX40, FTSE100, and NAS100. Additional indices may be available in the MT4 platform.

indices.q: Can I trade indices on MetaTrader 4?
indices.a: Yes. All index CFDs are available through your Olla Trade MT4 account on desktop, web, iOS, and Android.
Stock market indices are influenced by corporate earnings, economic data (GDP, employment, inflation), central bank decisions, geopolitical events, and overall investor risk sentiment.

indices.q: Are index trading hours different by market?
indices.a: Yes. Index CFD trading hours generally follow the underlying exchange schedule. US indices follow New York market hours, while European indices follow their respective exchange sessions.

energies.q: What is the difference between Brent and WTI?
energies.a: Brent Crude is extracted from the North Sea and serves as the global benchmark. WTI is the North American benchmark — slightly lighter and sweeter. Brent typically trades at a slight premium to WTI.
Oil prices are driven by OPEC+ production decisions, US EIA inventory reports, geopolitical events in oil-producing regions, global growth data, USD strength, and seasonal demand factors.

energies.q: Can I trade oil without physical delivery?
energies.a: Yes. Olla Trade offers oil CFDs — you speculate on price movements without any physical delivery. You simply profit or lose based on price direction.

energies.q: What leverage is available on energy CFDs?
energies.a: Olla Trade offers leverage up to 1:100 on energy instruments. This amplifies both gains and losses in proportion to your position size.

crypto.a: Cryptocurrency CFDs allow you to speculate on the price of digital currencies without owning them. No wallet, no exchange account — trade directly from MT4.

crypto.q: Which cryptocurrencies are available on Olla Trade?
crypto.a: Olla Trade offers CFDs on BTC/USD (Bitcoin), ETH/USD (Ethereum), LTC/USD (Litecoin), and XRP/USD (Ripple).

crypto.q: Can I trade crypto without a digital wallet?
crypto.a: Yes. Olla Trade crypto CFDs do not require a digital wallet, exchange account, or actual cryptocurrency ownership.

crypto.q: What are the trading hours for crypto CFDs?
crypto.a: Olla Trade cryptocurrency CFDs are available Monday to Friday. Underlying crypto markets operate 24/7, so significant weekend price gaps may occur when CFDs reopen Monday.

crypto.q: What leverage is available on cryptocurrencies?
crypto.a: Olla Trade offers leverage up to 1:10 on cryptocurrency CFDs. Given high crypto volatility, strong risk management is essential.

stocks.a: A stock CFD allows you to speculate on the price of a company's share without owning the underlying stock.

stocks.q: Do I receive dividends on stock CFDs?
stocks.a: Stock CFD traders may receive dividend adjustments (credited or debited) when a company pays a dividend, depending on long or short position at the ex-dividend date.

stocks.q: What leverage is available on stock CFDs?
stocks.a: Olla Trade offers leverage up to 1:10 on stock CFDs. Conservative position sizing and stop-loss orders are strongly recommended.

stocks.q: Can I short a stock with Olla Trade?
stocks.a: Yes. With stock CFDs you can go short if you believe a company's share price will fall.

stocks.q: What hours can I trade stocks?
stocks.a: Stock CFD trading follows the hours of the underlying exchange (NYSE/NASDAQ for US stocks, LSE for UK stocks). Check your MT4 instrument specifications for current hours.

execution.a: Market execution means your orders are filled at the best available market price at the time the order is processed. Olla Trade uses market execution across all account types.

execution.q: Can slippage occur when trading with Olla Trade?
execution.a: Yes. Slippage can occur during periods of high market volatility, major economic news releases, or low liquidity conditions. Slippage can be positive (filled at a better price) or negative (filled at a worse price).

Olla Trade pricing is derived from aggregated quotes provided by our liquidity providers. The prices displayed in MetaTrader 4 reflect the best available bid and ask prices from our liquidity pool at any given moment.

Execution quality can be influenced by: current market volatility, available liquidity for the instrument being traded, the size of the order relative to available market depth, network connection latency, and the time of day and active trading sessions.

execution.q: What happens during volatile market conditions?
execution.a: During periods of high volatility — such as major economic data releases, central bank announcements, or significant geopolitical events — market prices can move rapidly and liquidity may temporarily reduce.

Yes. Spreads are variable and may widen significantly during periods of high market volatility, outside of regular market trading hours, around major economic news releases, or during periods of reduced market liquidity.

platform.a: MetaTrader 4 (MT4) is the world's most widely used retail trading platform, developed by MetaQuotes. It offers advanced charting, 30+ built-in technical indicators, 9 chart types, automated trading via Expert Advisors (EAs), and direct market access. Olla Trade provides MT4 access across desktop, web, iOS, and Android.

platform.q: Can I use MT4 on desktop and mobile?
platform.a: Yes. MT4 is available on Windows desktop (full-featured client), WebTrader (access from any modern browser with no installation required), iOS (App Store), and Android (Google Play). All versions connect to the same Olla Trade account in real time.

Yes. MetaTrader 4 has full support for automated trading through Expert Advisors written in MQL4. You can import custom EAs, use the built-in strategy tester to backtest against historical data, and run EAs live on your Olla Trade account.

platform.q: How do I log in to MT4 with my Olla Trade account?
platform.a: Your MT4 login credentials (account number, password, and server address) are sent to you by email when your Olla Trade account is approved. Open MT4, go to File > Login to Trade Account, enter your credentials, and select the Olla Trade server from the list.

MT4 Desktop for Windows is available for download from the Olla Trade website and from the official MetaQuotes website. The iOS app is on the Apple App Store and the Android version is on Google Play. WebTrader requires no download.

conditions.hero_subtitle: Competitive spreads, flexible leverage, and market execution across 500+ instruments. All conditions are disclosed upfront — no hidden fees, no surprises.
conditions.overview_desc: Olla Trade uses market execution across all accounts. Orders are filled at the best available market price at the time of submission, subject to available liquidity and market conditions.

overview_cards.title: Variable Spreads
overview_cards.body: Spreads are variable and reflect live market conditions. Major Forex pairs start from 0.0 pips on the Elite account and from 1.4 pips on the Standard account. Spreads may widen during major economic announcements, market open/close, or periods of reduced liquidity.

overview_cards.title: Market Execution
overview_cards.body: All orders are executed at the best available market price. No dealer intervention occurs in the order execution process. Expert Advisors (EAs) and automated trading strategies are fully supported on all account types.

overview_cards.title: Flexible Leverage
overview_cards.body: Leverage up to 1:500 is available on Forex pairs. Leverage varies by instrument class. Higher leverage increases both potential gains and losses. Clients should use leverage carefully and in accordance with their risk tolerance.

overview_cards.title: Negative Balance Protection
overview_cards.body: Olla Trade applies negative balance protection to all client accounts. In most market conditions, your losses will not exceed your deposited balance. Where an account falls into a negative balance due to exceptional market events, Olla Trade will reset the balance to zero at no charge.

overview_cards.title: No Requotes
overview_cards.body: The market execution model means orders are not subject to requotes. All orders are accepted and executed at prevailing market prices. Slippage — execution at a price different from the requested price — may occur in fast-moving markets.

overview_cards.title: EA & Scalping Allowed
overview_cards.body: Expert Advisors, scalping strategies, news trading, and hedging are permitted on all Olla Trade accounts. There are no restrictions on trading styles. All strategies must remain within the bounds of the account terms and conditions.

conditions.accounts_desc: Three account types designed for different experience levels and capital sizes — all on MetaTrader 4.
conditions.accounts_note: * Spreads are variable. Figures shown are indicative minimum spreads under normal market conditions. Actual spreads may be wider during low-liquidity periods or major news events.
conditions.spreads_note: Spreads are variable and depend on market liquidity. They may widen during low-liquidity sessions, major economic events, or market open and close periods.
conditions.leverage_desc1: Leverage allows traders to control a larger position with a smaller amount of capital. Olla Trade offers leverage up to 1:500 on Forex pairs. Leverage is not fixed — different instruments have different maximum leverage limits based on risk characteristics.
conditions.leverage_desc2: Higher leverage amplifies both potential profits and potential losses. Clients are responsible for maintaining sufficient margin in their accounts. Olla Trade does not guarantee that margin calls will always prevent losses exceeding the account balance under all market conditions.
conditions.margin_warning_body: When your account equity falls to 80% of the required margin (Margin Call level), you will receive a margin call notification. At 20% (Stop Out level), positions may begin to be closed automatically to protect remaining equity. Clients are responsible for monitoring margin levels at all times.
conditions.exec_desc: All Olla Trade accounts use market execution. Orders are transmitted directly to liquidity providers at the best available price.

exec_cards.title: Market Execution
exec_cards.desc: Orders are filled at the best available market price at the time of execution — no dealer confirmation required.

exec_cards.title: No Requotes
exec_cards.desc: The market execution model eliminates requotes. Every order submitted is accepted and filled at prevailing market prices.

exec_cards.title: Slippage Disclosure
exec_cards.desc: Execution price may differ from the requested price, particularly during fast-moving markets or major economic releases. Slippage can be positive or negative.

exec_cards.title: EA Support
exec_cards.desc: Fully supports Expert Advisors, automated strategies, scalping, hedging, and all standard MT4 trading approaches.

order_items.name: Market Order
order_items.desc: Execute immediately at the current best available market price.

order_items.name: Limit Order
order_items.desc: Execute at a specified price or better. Used to enter or exit at a pre-defined level.

order_items.name: Stop Order
order_items.desc: Triggered when price reaches a specified level, then executed as a market order.

order_items.name: Stop Loss
order_items.desc: Automatically closes a position at a defined price to limit potential loss.

order_items.name: Take Profit
order_items.desc: Automatically closes a position at a defined price when a profit target is reached.

order_items.name: Trailing Stop
order_items.desc: A dynamic stop loss that follows price movements, locking in profit as the market moves in your favour.

conditions.hours_desc: Olla Trade follows standard international market hours. All times are in server time (GMT+2 during daylight saving, GMT+3 in winter).
conditions.hours_note: Trading hours may be affected by public holidays and extraordinary market events. Spreads may widen at market open and close. Check your MT4 platform for live session information.

instruments_desc.desc: Major, minor, and exotic currency pairs including EUR/USD, GBP/USD, USD/JPY, USD/CHF, and more.
Gold (XAU/USD), Silver (XAG/USD), and other precious metals via CFDs.
Global stock indices including US30, SPX500, NAS100, GER30, UK100, and more.
Oil (Brent & WTI), Natural Gas, and other energy CFDs.
Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), Litecoin (LTC), Ripple (XRP), and other major crypto CFDs.
Single stock CFDs from the US, UK, EU, and other major markets including Apple, Amazon, Tesla, and more.

conditions.risk_p1: Trading Forex and CFDs involves significant risk of loss and may not be suitable for all investors. Leveraged trading amplifies both potential profits and potential losses — you could lose more than your initial deposit.
conditions.risk_p2: Spreads are variable and may widen during periods of market volatility, reduced liquidity, or major economic announcements. Slippage may occur during fast-moving markets. Past performance is not indicative of future results.
conditions.risk_p3: You should ensure you understand how CFDs work and whether you can afford to take the risk. Please read our
conditions.cta_subtitle: Open an account in minutes and access 500+ instruments with tight spreads, fast execution, and full MetaTrader 4 support.

accounts.hero_subtitle: Compare trading conditions, spreads, execution, and features across all Olla Trade account types.
accounts.compare_subtitle: Detailed breakdown of all conditions across every account type.

faqs.q: Which account is best for beginners?
faqs.a: The Standard account is ideal for beginners — $10 minimum deposit, no commission, full MT4 access, and 500+ instruments.

faqs.q: Can I upgrade my account type later?
faqs.a: Yes. You can upgrade from Standard to Pro or Elite by depositing the required minimum. Contact support to arrange your upgrade.
Yes. A demo account is available for all account types so you can practise with virtual funds before going live.

faqs.q: What is the difference between variable and raw spreads?
faqs.a: Variable spreads fluctuate with market conditions. Raw spreads on the Elite account are the direct interbank rate plus a fixed commission per lot — typically lower in liquid market conditions.

faqs.q: How quickly can I open an account?
faqs.a: Registration takes about 2 minutes. KYC verification typically takes 1–2 business days. You can trade once approved.

funding.methods_subtitle: Fund your Olla Trade account securely using major cryptocurrencies. All methods are accepted with no fees from Olla Trade.
funding.support_note: Additional payment methods may be available in your client portal.
funding.flow_desc: Every deposit and withdrawal is processed through your personal SSL-encrypted client portal. Your funds move directly from your wallet to your trading account via the blockchain — no intermediaries, no hidden steps.

flow_features.label: No Olla Trade Fees
flow_features.desc: We charge zero fees on deposits or withdrawals. Network fees apply on the blockchain side only.

flow_features.label: Multiple Crypto Methods
flow_features.desc: USDT TRC20, USDT ERC20, Bitcoin, and Ethereum — with more methods available in the client portal.

flow_features.label: Credited After Confirmations
flow_features.desc: Funds appear in your trading account automatically after the required blockchain confirmations.

flow_features.label: Same-Method Withdrawals
flow_features.desc: Withdrawals are returned to the same method used for deposit — a standard security requirement.

deposit_steps: Log in to your Olla Trade client portal.
Access your account at direct.ollatrade.com using your credentials.
In your portal dashboard, find the Funding or Deposit section.
Copy the deposit address and send funds.
Send the exact crypto amount to the provided wallet address.
Your account balance updates automatically after the required blockchain confirmations.
withdraw_steps: Log in to your client portal.
Access your account at direct.ollatrade.com using your credentials.
In your portal dashboard, find the Withdrawal section.
Choose the same method used for your original deposit.
Input the withdrawal amount and your wallet address or bank details.
Withdrawal requests are reviewed and processed within 24–48 business hours after approval.
funding.times_note: * Processing times are estimates only and may vary depending on verification status, payment method, and transaction volume. Blockchain network fees may apply.

rules.title: Account Verification Required
rules.desc: Withdrawal requests require completed KYC/AML account verification. Ensure your identity documents and proof of address are submitted and approved.

rules.title: Submit via Client Portal Only
rules.desc: All withdrawal requests must be submitted through your official Olla Trade client portal. Requests made via email or chat cannot be accepted for security reasons.

rules.title: Blockchain Confirmations
rules.desc: Crypto deposits are credited after sufficient blockchain network confirmations. Processing times vary by network and current congestion conditions.

rules.title: Additional Documents May Be Requested
rules.desc: Olla Trade may request additional documentation for security, compliance, or anti-money laundering verification purposes before processing your withdrawal.

rules.title: Bonus Credit Withdrawal
rules.desc: Bonus trading credit cannot be withdrawn directly unless explicitly stated in the relevant promotion's official terms and conditions.

rules.title: Same Method Policy
rules.desc: Where possible, withdrawals are processed back to the same method used for the original deposit. This is a standard financial security requirement.

security_features.title: Secure Client Portal
security_features.desc: All funding and withdrawal operations are conducted through your SSL-encrypted Olla Trade client portal — never via email or third-party links.

security_features.title: Identity Verification (KYC)
security_features.desc: Account verification ensures that only the registered account holder can request or receive funds, protecting you from unauthorised withdrawals.

security_features.title: Transaction History
security_features.desc: A complete record of all deposits, withdrawals, and transactions is available in your client portal at all times for your review.

security_features.title: Compliance Review
security_features.desc: All transactions are subject to standard compliance and anti-money laundering screening in line with applicable financial regulations.

security_features.title: Segregated Client Funds
security_features.desc: Client funds are held separately from company operational funds, providing an additional layer of financial security for your deposits.

security_features.title: Processing Oversight
security_features.desc: Withdrawal requests are reviewed and approved by our operations team during business hours to ensure accuracy and security before processing.

funding.cta_subtitle: Log in to your client portal to make a deposit or withdrawal request.

execution.crosslink_text: Looking for the formal legal Order Execution Policy document?
execution.intro_p1: Olla Trade operates as an execution-only service using a market execution model. When you place a trade, your order is processed through our trading infrastructure and submitted to available liquidity at the best price achievable under current market conditions.
execution.intro_p2: Prices displayed in MetaTrader 4 are derived from aggregated quotes provided by our liquidity providers. These prices are variable and reflect real-time market conditions including supply and demand, overall market liquidity, and the time of the active trading session.
execution.intro_p3: Execution quality may vary depending on market conditions. Olla Trade does not provide investment advice and does not guarantee execution at any specific price. Orders are subject to available liquidity and prevailing market conditions at the time of execution.

intro_cards.title: Market Execution
intro_cards.desc: Orders filled at best available market price in real time, subject to liquidity.

intro_cards.title: Global Markets
intro_cards.desc: Access to Forex, Metals, Indices, Energies, Crypto, and Stocks — all through MT4.

intro_cards.title: MT4 Infrastructure
intro_cards.desc: All orders processed through MetaTrader 4 — industry-standard trading platform.

intro_cards.title: Variable Pricing
intro_cards.desc: Prices derived from liquidity providers and subject to prevailing market conditions.

execution.flow_desc: When you submit an order in MetaTrader 4, it is transmitted to Olla Trade's execution infrastructure, validated against available margin and risk parameters, then routed to our liquidity pool. The best available price is selected and the fill confirmation is returned to your terminal.

flow_features.label: STP Routing
flow_features.desc: Orders are routed straight-through to liquidity providers without manual dealer intervention under normal conditions.

flow_features.label: Best Available Price
flow_features.desc: Our pricing engine aggregates quotes from multiple liquidity providers to achieve the best available bid and ask at time of execution.

flow_features.label: Execution Subject to Liquidity
flow_features.desc: Final execution price depends on available market liquidity and conditions at the moment the order is processed.

flow_features.label: No Requotes
flow_features.desc: Market execution means all orders are accepted at the prevailing price — you will not be asked to confirm a different price.

execution.model_desc: Understanding how different order types are processed. Execution quality may vary depending on market conditions and available liquidity.

model_cards.title: Market Execution
model_cards.desc: Market orders are filled at the best available price at the time of execution. The final price may differ from the quoted price at the moment the order was placed, particularly in fast-moving market conditions.

model_cards.title: Pending Orders
model_cards.desc: Pending orders instruct the platform to open a position when the market reaches a specified price. Types include Buy/Sell Limit and Buy/Sell Stop. Execution at the exact specified price is subject to available market liquidity.

model_cards.title: Stop Loss Orders
model_cards.desc: A Stop Loss order automatically closes a position at a specified loss level. Stop Loss orders help limit potential losses but do not guarantee closure at the exact requested price, particularly during rapid price movements or market gaps.

model_cards.title: Take Profit Orders
model_cards.desc: A Take Profit order closes a position automatically when the market reaches a specified profit target. Execution is subject to available market liquidity at the time the target price is reached.

model_cards.title: Slippage
model_cards.desc: Slippage refers to the difference between the expected price of a trade and the price at which the trade is actually executed. Slippage can occur in fast-moving or low-liquidity market conditions and can be positive or negative.

model_cards.title: No Requote Policy
model_cards.desc: Olla Trade does not issue requotes on client orders. Under the market execution model, all orders are accepted and executed at the best available prevailing market price at the time of submission.

execution.slippage_desc: Slippage is a normal and expected feature of trading in real financial markets. It occurs when the market moves between the moment you submit an order and the moment it is processed by our execution engine.

slippage_cards.title: When Slippage Occurs
slippage_cards.desc: Slippage is most common during high market volatility, major economic news releases, and periods of low liquidity — such as outside regular trading hours or around market open/close.

slippage_cards.title: Positive Slippage
slippage_cards.desc: Positive slippage occurs when your order is filled at a better price than requested — for example, a buy order fills lower than the requested price. This benefits the client.

slippage_cards.title: Negative Slippage
slippage_cards.desc: Negative slippage occurs when your order is filled at a worse price than requested. This can happen during rapid market movements or when large orders consume available liquidity at the requested price.

slippage_cards.title: Managing Slippage
slippage_cards.desc: Use limit orders instead of market orders where possible. Avoid trading immediately around major economic data releases. Monitor the economic calendar and apply appropriate position sizing.

execution.infra_desc: Olla Trade's trading infrastructure is built for reliable, continuous order execution across all instruments and sessions.

infra_cards.title: Server Location
infra_cards.desc: Trading servers are hosted in professional data centres with redundant connectivity and power systems for maximum uptime.

infra_cards.title: Liquidity Aggregation
infra_cards.desc: Prices are sourced from multiple institutional liquidity providers. The best available bid and ask prices are aggregated and presented to clients in real time.

infra_cards.title: MT4 Bridge
infra_cards.desc: Client orders placed via MetaTrader 4 are transmitted instantly through our MT4 bridge to our execution infrastructure for processing.

infra_cards.title: Continuous Monitoring
infra_cards.desc: Our operations team monitors execution quality, server performance, and connectivity on an ongoing basis during all trading sessions.

execution.pricing_desc: Olla Trade derives all prices from aggregated quotes provided by our institutional liquidity providers. Prices reflect real-time market conditions and are passed through to clients without any artificial mark-up beyond the disclosed spread.
pricing_points: Prices are aggregated from multiple liquidity providers in real time.
The best available bid and ask prices are presented to clients via MetaTrader 4.
Spreads are variable and reflect prevailing market liquidity and session activity.
Olla Trade earns revenue from the spread between bid and ask prices.
No additional hidden charges are applied to standard trading activity.
All pricing methodology is disclosed in the Order Execution Policy.
execution.cta_title: Open an Account with Olla Trade
execution.cta_subtitle: Access 500+ instruments with market execution, tight spreads, and full MetaTrader 4 support.
execution.order_types_desc: MetaTrader 4 supports a range of order types to help you manage your trading positions. All order types are subject to prevailing market conditions and available liquidity.

order_types.name: Market Order
order_types.desc: Executed immediately at the best available current market price. The fill price may differ from the quoted price at the moment the order is placed.

order_types.name: Buy/Sell Limit
order_types.desc: Pending order to open at a more favourable price than current market. A Buy Limit opens below current price; a Sell Limit opens above.

order_types.name: Buy/Sell Stop
order_types.desc: Pending order to open beyond the current market level. A Buy Stop triggers above current price; a Sell Stop triggers below.

order_types.name: Stop Loss
order_types.desc: Automatically closes a losing position when the market reaches a specified level. Execution is not guaranteed at the exact price during rapid price movements.

order_types.name: Take Profit
order_types.desc: Automatically closes a profitable position when the market reaches a target price. Subject to market conditions and available liquidity at the time of execution.

platform.section_title: The World's Most Popular Trading Platform
platform.section_desc: Advanced charting, automated trading, and real-time execution across all global markets — on every device you own. Download or access via browser with no installation required.

features.title: 30+ Technical Indicators
features.desc: Built-in MA, MACD, RSI, Bollinger Bands, Ichimoku, and 25+ more indicator types. All with full customisation and overlay capability.

features.title: Expert Advisors (EAs)
features.desc: Full MQL4 automated trading support. Backtest strategies on historical price data before going live.

features.title: Custom Alerts
features.desc: Price, indicator, and margin alerts via MT4 desktop, mobile push, or email.

features.title: Multi-Chart Layout
features.desc: Monitor multiple instruments and timeframes simultaneously on a single screen.

features.title: 9 Chart Timeframes
features.desc: M1 through MN — candlestick, bar, and line chart types available.

features.title: One-Click Execution
features.desc: Direct chart trading with instant market order submission — no dialog required.

features.title: Full MQL4 Support
features.desc: Build, import, and run automated strategies written in MetaQuotes Language 4.

features.title: VPS Compatible
features.desc: Run MT4 and EAs on a Virtual Private Server for 24/5 uninterrupted trading.

platform.cta_subtitle: Open a free account and access MT4 on desktop, WebTrader, iOS, and Android with professional trading conditions.
platform.cross_title: One Account. Every Device. Always Synced.
platform.cross_desc: Your Olla Trade MT4 account is fully synchronized across every platform. Open a position on desktop, monitor it on mobile, and manage it from your tablet — all in real time.

cross_items.d: Full MetaTrader 4 with all features: multi-chart layout, full EA support, backtesting, and local alerts.
Access your full MT4 account from any modern browser — no installation required. Works on Mac, Linux, and Chromebook.
The official MT4 mobile app gives you real-time quotes, interactive charts, one-tap trading, and push notifications.
Positions, account balance, chart settings, and watchlists stay synchronized across all your devices automatically.

platform.all_desc: Everything a professional trader needs — built into the world's most trusted trading platform.
platform.faq_subtitle: Common questions about MetaTrader 4, account setup, Expert Advisors, and platform compatibility.
